title: Spread & Rest Operators ... 💥
description: Combine massive objects and clone arrays flawlessly.

slides.title: The Exploding Array 💥
Cloning and Combining 🧬
Spreading Objects 🗄️
Try It Out 💻
slides.content: Notice how spreading creates a PERFECT 1-to-1 clone of an object, leaving the fragile original perfectly untouched and safe!

quizData.question: When spreading an object inside a NEW object `let cloned = { ...user, name: "Bob" }`, why did 'Bob' overwrite the original name?

options.text: Because spreading deletes the original completely
Because 'Bob' comes AFTER the spread. In JavaScript objects, the very last duplicated Key always wins cleanly.

quizData.explanation: If an object contains {name: 'A', name: 'B'}, it only keeps the very final (latest) entry! Thus spreading, then writing a new key, beautifully overwrites the cloned data.

slides.title: The Rest Operator 🗑️

quizData.question: What is the core difference between Spread and Rest (even though they share the exact `...` syntax)?

options.text: There is no difference, they are identical
Spread explodes an Array OUT into open items. Rest gathers open items IN to form an Array.
Spread is for Arrays, Rest is for Objects

quizData.explanation: Spread = Unpacking a box scattering it across the floor. Rest = Looking at loose items on the floor, packing them neatly back into an 'Array' box.
